design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → engage in 1v1 or 2v2 combat → execute combos and special moves → defeat opponent via health depletion or time-out → advance through tournament ladder with escalating difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastery of character-specific move sets and combo timing versus opponent prediction and spacing control; execution precision under pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a martial arts champion in the Mortal Kombat tournament; execute devastating combos and brutal finishing moves (Fatalities, Stage Fatalities, Brutalities)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: UMK3 is opinionated about combo accessibility and balance: chain combos can be initiated from jump attacks (vertical or angled), lowering execution barriers; damage scaling was deliberately applied to some combos to prevent oppressive strategies. The game also emphasizes character roster inclusivity by restoring fan-favorite characters (Reptile, Kitana, Jade, Scorpion) removed from MK3, signaling that character selection matters narratively and mechanically. The introduction of 2-on-2 and 8-player tournament modes positions the game as both arcade-cabinet spectacle and competitive tournament engine.

difficulty.lives_system: Single elimination per fight; player must defeat opponent to advance
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system allows restart after defeat in arcade/tournament modes
difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament progression is linear; defeating each opponent advances player through bracket toward Shao Kahn final boss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extra Master difficulty tier available beyond standard difficulty settings; Kombat Kodes (cheat/configuration passwords) allow access to normally-locked stages and modifiers, giving players control over challenge parameters

progression.unlock_mechanism: Completion of main tournament or 8-player tournament mode unlocks Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures—selectable prize rewards including additional fights, cutscenes, and other unlockable content. Kombat Kodes unlock hidden stages and configuration options without traditional progression gating.
replayability_hooks: Character-specific ending cinematics encourage replay with different roster members
8-player tournament mode provides alternate progression path
Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures offer variable post-game content
Multiple stages and stage-specific fatalities incentivize different arena selection
2-on-2 multiplayer mode (human players) extends competitive replayability
Kombat Kode configurations create alternate rule sets for replays

world_building_techniques: Character ending sequences featuring story resolution after tournament victory
Stage environments (Scorpion's Lair/Hell, Jade's Desert, Kahn's Kave) reinforce the Mortal Kombat tournament setting and visual mythology
Stage Fatalities tie environment design to narrative (e.g., lava river in Scorpion's Lair)
Character biographies shown in attract mode for roster additions (Kitana, Jade, Scorpion, Reptile)
Background environmental storytelling (Cyrax stuck in sand in Jade's Desert references his MK3 story ending)
Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures cutscenes provide post-tournament narrative payoff

boss_design.philosophy: Shao Kahn functions as final tournament boss with escalated AI and moveset; boss fights are presented as the narrative climax of the tournament progression. The boss represents the antagonistic force that corrupted the martial arts tournament.
notable_bosses: Shao Kahn (final boss): accessible via tournament completion; features improved AI and stage-specific interactions

faction_indicators: Returning ninja characters (Reptile, Scorpion) signal restoration of warrior clans
Kitana and Jade represent royal court members with political allegiances
Tournament structure implies organized martial arts hierarchy
danger_signals: Shao Kahn's dominion over tournament and realm
Hell/Scorpion's Lair stage indicates infernal corruption
Stage Fatality mechanics (lava deaths) signal environmental lethality
environmental_storytelling: Multiple unique stage biomes (Hell, Desert, Cavern, Waterfront) establish a varied contested realm
Stage transitions between UMK3 exclusive backgrounds and original MK3 locations suggest a unified tournament world
NPC background details (Cyrax in sand) suggest ongoing world events beyond playable character narratives
